tags: Courts noblewoman connected to powerful figure in home city.
Accepts diplomatic exile rather than face punishment.
Travels to foreign lands by caravan with mentor.
Takes refuge with foreigners after raiders attack caravan.
Witnesses foreign king's funeral and ritual sacrifice.
Observes leader kill rival in self-defense, establishing succession.
Reluctantly accepts selection into warband by oracle's prophecy.
Prepares foreign mount for journey despite warriors' mockery.
Learns foreign language through patient observation around campfire.
Demonstrates skilled horsemanship to silence mocking warriors.
Teaches leader to write in foreign script at leader's request.
Declines drink violating religious law, accepts permitted alternative.
Travels by sea and land with warband to besieged kingdom.
Searches raided dwelling and discovers enemy's cult icon.
Fights in hall during first enemy attack, surviving the night.
Struggles to lift heavy foreign weapon given by companions.
Reforges foreign weapon into familiar cultural style.
Demonstrates reforged weapon's effectiveness through cutting test.
Reasons out assault strategy through indirect question to companion.
Fights in multiple defensive clashes against enemy raiders.
Rides out during battle to rescue civilian from enemy.
Swims through underwater passages to infiltrate enemy stronghold.
Fights through enemy lair alongside surviving companions.
Escapes enemy lair after cult leader killed, leaving fallen companion.
Returns to settlement to prepare fortifications for last stand.
Prays for forgiveness before final battle.
Joins foreign warriors in reciting their death prayer.
Fights in final battle as enemy assault is broken.
Witnesses leader's death and funeral alongside survivors.
Departs for home, vowing to record leader's deeds.
Receives classical education in letters and verse as a child.
Trains in athletics and horsemanship during youth.
Follows father into civic magistracy in home city.
Consults oracle about how to gain renown.
Debases state currency through fraud or corruption.
Flees or is exiled after scandal is discovered.
Travels to great city as exile and stranger.
Derives philosophical lesson from observing simple animal.
Seeks out harsh teacher despite repeated refusal.
Endures threat of violence until teacher accepts him.
Becomes pupil through sheer persistence.
Reduces garments to single multipurpose cloak.
Reduces possessions to single bag for food.
Takes up residence in discarded vessel or structure.
Subjects body to extreme heat as hardening exercise.
Subjects body to extreme cold as hardening exercise.
Goes barefoot through city as ascetic discipline.
Dismisses escaped servant with philosophical indifference.
Supports self by begging in public spaces.
Performs theatrical search for genuine virtue in crowded market.
Discards possession after seeing simpler alternative.
Discards another possession after seeing even simpler method.
Practices receiving refusal by begging from inanimate object.
Performs shocking bodily act publicly to make philosophical point.
Eats, sleeps, and converses publicly without shame.
Answers insult with deliberate bodily degradation of insulter.
Insults wealthy host's dignity while accepting hospitality.
Lures crowd with promise, then punishes them for failing test.
Deliberately invites violence through provocative appearance.
Publicly shames attackers until they are disgraced.
Attempts extreme ascetic practice but fails physically.
Moves against crowd flow as deliberate life practice.
Tramples rival philosopher's luxury, denouncing vanity.
Mocks rival's abstract definition with crude literal example.
Demands concrete proof of rival's abstract philosophical concepts.
Tests rival's appetite, then mocks resulting hypocrisy.
Critiques rival's foreign luxury-seeking as pointless.
Visits sick rival and delivers cutting observation.
Dismisses rival's teaching as worthless.
Tells rival that simple labor beats courting powerful.
Positions self near danger to mock incompetent practitioner.
Refutes abstract argument through demonstrative action.
Disrupts pompous speech with distracting mundane act.
Disrupts lecture by producing absurd counter-spectacle.
Answers request for identification with obscene gesture.
Mocks religious worship through satirical dedication.
Tests potential student with humiliating task; rejects failure.
Warns pious worshipper of absurdity in their posture.
Defaces monument with inscription critiquing its source.
Annotates public inscription with cynical observation.
Mocks weak city's pretensions to security.
Refuses religious initiation, preferring virtue to afterlife promise.
Declines foreign patron's invitation, preferring poverty at home.
Retaliates violently after being struck unprovoked.
Is captured by pirates during voyage.
Counsels fellow captives to accept fate with philosophical calm.
Selects own buyer at slave auction, claiming to be master.
Declares to auctioneer that skill is governing men.
Takes charge of owner's household and children's education as slave.
Teaches owner's children martial and athletic skills.
Has children memorize classical works and own teachings.
Trains children in ascetic self-sufficiency and simple living.
Settles near gymnasium in new city after gaining freedom.
Crowns self victor at games with improvised wreath.
Attends public games, claims to compete against human weakness itself.
Protests champion's victory, claiming higher form of contest.
Refuses to join dignitaries in greeting conquering ruler.
Remains at leisure while powerful ruler approaches.
Dismisses conqueror's offer by requesting only removal of shadow.
Answers conqueror's self-announcement with own unyielding identity.
Is captured in battle and brought before conquering king.
Reframes captivity as surveillance upon captor's vice; is released.
Mocks powerful figure's death threat with contemptuous comparison.
Insults messenger with wordplay on messenger's own name.
Adopts walking stick as concession to age while traveling.
Travels between various cities in later years.
Mocks admired people about their failures despite respecting them.
Observes society valuing animals over children and remarks upon it.
Instructs that corpse be given to nature without ceremony.
Dies under ambiguous or disputed circumstances.
Leaves followers disputing how to honor remains.
Quarrels with foster-brother king, exiled for treason.
Writes book chronicling explorations through foreign wilderness.
Enters rival nation's service as advisor to king.
Assists king during perilous sorcerous conjuring.
Fights summoned horror during ritual, hewing regenerating limbs.
Helps complete sorcery banishing enemy hero to cursed prison.
Reports to noblewoman on battle aftermath and court intrigues.
Advises king on strategy, warns against trusting rival general.
Serves as noblewoman's escort at court functions.
Sails with invasion fleet to enemy homeland.

Travels alone, encounters strange wonders in wilderness.
Develops close friendship with noblewoman, composes serenade.
Falls in love with enemy noblewoman during occupation.
Betrays own side, helps beloved escape after siege.

Is confronted and denounced for betrayals mid-battle.

Commands magical dwelling to halt for her

Convinces apprentice to escort her to warn sibling

Petitions ruler on master's behalf to damage his reputation

Agrees to hide cursed person's presence from master
Helps relocate bound spirit during dwelling's emergency move

Commands enchanted pursuer to leave when it finds her
Sends romantic rival away when she visits dwelling
Reveals to master that new animal is actually cursed person
Tends grounds with cursed person using magical tools
Argues with master and vows to leave dwelling next day

Participates in festival preparations on sacred holiday
Receives visits from stepmother, siblings, and mentor figures
Uses magical transport to race into enemy territory for rescue
Gets captured by antagonist in enemy stronghold
Witnesses antagonist's plan to create composite being from victims
Commands enchanted implement to attack hidden enemy
Saves bound spirit when enemy drops it during confrontation
Speaks life into bound spirit, breaking magical contract
Removes master's heart from bound spirit's keeping

Returns to true form as curse finally breaks
Admits love for master and commits to shared future
Escapes kitchen during drunken celebration of heir's birth
Follows rival servant through corridors hoping to rise in station
